_comment: Decision & roadmap board. Edit items here; roadmap.html renders/filters them. status: proposed | in-progress | blocked | decided | done. owner: 'you' (needs a human decision/action) or 'claude' (I can execute).

items.title: Adopt this revamp as the StatsUpAI site direction
items.detail: Replace the 2014 Bootstrap template, or keep this as a parallel preview. Tracked in issue #4 for collaborator input.

items.title: Grant repo access to Statsup-AI/statsupai.github.io
items.detail: Canonical _data/webinars.yml has full structured talk data (date/speaker/affiliation/series/abstract/bio/registration). One-time allowlist add = permanent fix for the webinar archive.

items.title: Exhaustive Gmail sweep to rebuild webinar archive
items.detail: No new access needed. Parse all stats-up-ai@googlegroups.com mail incl. digests, 2024→now, and reconcile events.json to best-available fidelity.

items.title: LinkedIn/X reference-link paste workflow
items.detail: assets/data/reference-links.json: paste post URLs per talk; individual LinkedIn/X post URLs are fetchable and get cross-checked on the next sweep. Company/profile pages are login-walled and not enumerable.

items.title: Separate 'Workshops & Recorded Events' archive section
items.detail: Most YouTube uploads are the 2025 BIRS workshop + conference showcases, NOT webinars. Keep Events = the 2 webinar series; add a links-only archive for BIRS/NIH AD Summit/Neuroimaging Conf. Buildable accurately now.

items.title: Keep Events scoped to the two webinar series
items.detail: Recommended & in effect: Health Data Science + GenAI×Stats only. One-off workshops belong in the archive (D5), matching the source site's own model.

items.title: Back-fill webinar gaps: Ombao, Tudorascu, Z. Zhang seminar
items.detail: Dates known (Jun 6 2025 / Jul 16 2025 / Sep 2025) but talk titles not in public snapshots. Needs canonical webinars.yml or Gmail sweep to add without guessing.

items.title: Map topic-titled Health-series recordings to speakers
items.detail: YouTube 'Seminar -- fMRI/ADRD/EEG/…' uploads are the Health series recordings; YouTube watch pages are IP-gated here so speaker↔video mapping needs the canonical list.

items.title: Confirmed recordings added (Çetinkaya-Rundel, Zou)
items.detail: Exact title matches to channel uploads; merged in #6.

items.title: Re-encode headshots to WebP + tighten asset budget
items.detail: No image tooling in this env. On a real runner: WebP/AVIF + srcset, then drop MAX_ASSET in check.mjs from 900KB → ~120KB.

items.title: Generate raster og.png from og.svg
items.detail: Some scrapers ignore SVG OG images. Needs image tooling / a CI step on a real runner.

items.title: Promote to statsupai.org domain root
items.detail: Activates robots.txt / sitemap.xml / 404.html (inert in the /statsupai-revamp/ subfolder).

items.title: Enable analytics
items.detail: Set MEASUREMENT_ID in assets/js/analytics.js (original used G-ZF2MZCZ03F). Off by default; prod-host-only; honors DNT.

items.title: Official branding assets (logo, colors)
items.detail: Currently a clean teal placeholder + SVG wordmark. Swap in official brand kit if one exists.

items.title: Community News: link-out vs. migrate Quarto posts
items.detail: Revamp links out to the Quarto feed. Decide whether to migrate long-form posts in natively.

items.title: Add abstracts & speaker bios to Events
items.detail: Announcement emails carry rich abstracts/bios; could expand each event card with a details disclosure.

items.title: Publish an .ics calendar feed file
items.detail: Per-event 'Add to calendar' exists; a single subscribe-able events.ics would let people follow the series.

items.title: Re-sync content/team from canonical repo
items.detail: Built from a mirror (snapshot ~2026-02-09). Datasets/articles/pipelines/team should be re-synced from Statsup-AI/statsupai.github.io. Depends on D2.

items.title: Interactive decision board (this page)
items.detail: roadmap.json + roadmap.html, filterable by status/category, JSON-validated by CI.

items.title: Site-wide search
items.detail: Per-section search exists on resource pages; a global search across datasets/articles/pipelines/events could help a large audience.
